entry.definition: A functional query language designed to query and transform collections of structured and unstructured data, typically XML.
A high-performance, full-featured text search engine library written in Java, used by eXist-db for full-text indexing and search.
A query language for selecting nodes from an XML document, also used in XSLT and XQuery for navigating document trees.
A hierarchical container in eXist-db that organizes XML documents and binary resources, similar to a directory in a filesystem.
Extensible Stylesheet Language Transformations, a language for transforming XML documents into other formats such as HTML, text, or other XML.
An index that tokenizes and stores text content to enable fast keyword searches, wildcard queries, phrase matching, and relevance-ranked results.
An index on typed values that enables fast equality, comparison, and range lookups without scanning all documents.
A character-level index that stores overlapping character sequences (n-grams) to enable fast substring matching and fuzzy search.
